Demo changes: key 1 faster, key 5 sweep's first tone faster, key 0 distinct from BOOM (shorter noise). DONE 2026-05-05: snd-beep shorter (300,30); snd-zap rewritten as smooth descending DO/+LOOP sweep; snd-chirp = three identical fast high blips with snd-pause silence between; snd-dock faster (600/300, dur 20); snd-boom now (60,500) using parameterized snd-noise(delay,duration); snd-sweep replaced with smooth ascending sweep via DO/+LOOP. snd-noise refactored to take (delay,duration) so caller can pick pitch. Added snd-pause helper (silence between bursts). Demo: key 1 faster (600,100); key 5 first tone faster (now smooth ascending); key 0 high-pitch hiss (1,400) distinct from BOOM. README gains complete cost tables (bytes + cycles + ms per word and per demo key).

summary: Kernel I: walk return stack to skip intervening colon-call frames
description: CODE_I currently reads from ,S flat (no walk), so a colon-defined helper called inside a DO loop sees the helper's own return address rather than the outer loop index.  Spacewarp pattern 'JOV-DMG I + C@' appears 13 times because the natural factoring ': jhp@ JOV-DMG I + C@ ;' returns the wrong value.  Standard ANS Forth I walks past colon-call frames; a one- or two-LEAS patch in CODE_I would restore the loop-body-helper factoring idiom and save ~65 bytes in spacewarp from this single pattern (plus similar wins elsewhere). DONE 2026-05-06: added VAR_LP loop-frame-pointer cell to kernel. CODE_DO now pushes (old_LP, limit, index) onto R-stack and updates VAR_LP via STS to point at the new index slot. CODE_LOOP / +LOOP / UNLOOP exit paths restore the saved old_LP and pop 6 bytes (was 4). CODE_I and CODE_J read via VAR_LP instead of fixed S offsets, so I works regardless of helper-frame depth and J chases one LP link. Verified via test-i.fs: direct-from-DO-body, from-colon-helper (was the bug), and J nested-loop all produce expected output. All existing demos rebuilt cleanly under both ROM and all-RAM kernels. Cost: kernel +52 bytes (3977 vs 3925 ROM); DO +6 cycles, LOOP/+LOOP exit +6, UNLOOP +6, I unchanged in cost, J +5.

summary: fc.py: hard-error (or fix) EXIT inside IF/THEN
description: Per CLAUDE.md, 'IF EXIT THEN' works as an early-return guard but 'IF ... EXIT ... THEN' with code after the EXIT silently miscompiles.  Currently we carry this as a defensive habit, but casual users hit it as an unexpected bug with quiet failure.  Either fix the compilation or detect the pattern at compile time and refuse it with a clear error. DONE 2026-05-06: fc.py parser now intercepts EXIT and emits one CFA_UNLOOP cell per active DO loop before CFA_EXIT. With the new VAR_LP-aware UNLOOP from #487, that pops the loop frame (index, limit, saved old_LP = 6 bytes) and restores VAR_LP cleanly. The standard idiom EXIT-inside-IF-inside-DO now compiles to correct code without forcing the author to remember UNLOOP. Removed the stale gotcha from CLAUDE.md, fc.py docstring, and src/sound/sound.fs comment. Verified via /tmp/test-exit2.fs (testB EXITs from inside DO with UNLOOP auto-inserted; testC then runs cleanly because R-stack is correct). All shipping demos rebuilt cleanly.

summary: fc.py: add +FIELD / RECORD struct-definer support
description: Apps currently express records via raw byte/word arrays and BASE +n C@ / +n ! patterns.  Spacewarp has many 'JOV-DMG i + C@' style accesses that a +FIELD-style definer would replace with named record fields.  Implement a Forth-83-style +FIELD primitive in fc.py (or a RECORD/END-RECORD pair) that emits constants for the field offsets.  Should be a modest fc.py change with a major source-readability payoff. DONE 2026-05-06: fc.py parser now recognises three Forth-2012-style struct definers: +FIELD (offset size --), CFIELD: (offset --, +1 byte), FIELD: (offset --, +2 bytes). Each defines the named word as a CONSTANT holding the field's offset, and replaces the cumulative-size cursor on the compile-time stack with offset+size so chained calls build a record layout. Pair with CONSTANT to capture total size: e.g. 0 CFIELD: ship.x CFIELD: ship.y FIELD: ship.vx FIELD: ship.vy CONSTANT /ship. Field words inline as literal offsets — zero call overhead. Documented in forth/tools/README.md and docs/reference.html. Verified via /tmp/test-fields.fs (offsets 0/1/2/4/6 emitted correctly; field reads/writes round-trip).

summary: fc.py: vocabularies / word lists
description: Single global namespace today.  Naming gets cramped fast in larger apps — spacewarp wanted helpers like c@1, w@+, and to redefine `0` etc. inside a private vocabulary.  Add WORDLIST or a lighter equivalent so apps can shadow short names internally and expose a curated public surface.  This is real architectural work, not a one-line fix; DEFERRED 2026-05-06: real architectural work, ~200 lines of fc.py changes for value we don't currently realize at ~28KB compiled across all demos. Keeping as future work — revisit when an app hits real naming-collision pain.

summary: lib/README.md: surface per-word bytes and cycle costs
description: lib/README.md is auto-generated by tools/gen-lib-readme.py and currently lists each public library word with its stack effect and kind. For a 64KB-ROM kernel where every byte and every CPU cycle matters, the two most-asked questions about a lib word are how big and how fast. fc.py already computes both internally (--cycles mode walks the call graph; item_size/sized compute byte costs). Plan: add --metrics-json PATH to fc.py emitting per-word {bytes, cy_min, cy_max, notes} JSON; new tools/gen-lib-metrics.py topo-sorts libs by Requires:, builds a unified stub, runs fc.py to produce build/lib-metrics.json; gen-lib-readme.py loads it and adds Bytes/Cycles/Notes columns to the word table. Wire into Makefile so make all keeps it fresh. DONE 2026-05-08: Added --metrics-json PATH to fc.py (writes per-word {bytes, cy_min, cy_max, notes} JSON across kernel_primitives, forth_words, code_words, kcode_words, variables). New tools/gen-lib-metrics.py topo-sorts lib/*.fs by Requires:+real INCLUDEs, writes a unified stub at build/lib-metrics.fs, runs fc.py to produce build/lib-metrics.json. tools/gen-lib-readme.py loads the JSON and adds Bytes/Cycles/Notes columns to each library word table (graceful fallback to empty cells when JSON missing). Wired through Makefile so make all keeps lib/README.md fresh. Verified: 66 forth + 9 CODE words across 20 libs all populated; spot-checked key-held? = 175cy = DOCOL(27)+swap(39)+kbd-scan(48)+and(39)+exit(22) and bytes=10=DOCOL+4 refs+EXIT; loopy words like rg-hline correctly tagged "has DO/LOOP"; CODE words like beam-restore-slice show per-iter notes from analyze_code_block. Existing demos (tetris, clock) still build cleanly with --cycles.

summary: lib/README.md: add per-word Description column
description: Word names in lib/README.md are mnemonic (cls-black, rg-pcls, snd-zap, expand-lo) and a reader scanning the index cannot tell what they do without opening the source. Plan: add a one-line Description column between Stack and Kind in the auto-generated table, sourced primarily from a new "\ word — desc" inline source-comment convention (placed in one of the 6 lines preceding a definition), falling back to docs/reference.html (already has 141 hand-authored one-liners in <td class="word-name"> rows; 79% coverage of current lib words). Case-insensitive name matching (KEY-HELD? ↔ key-held?). When source comment AND reference.html both exist and disagree, source wins and a docs-drift warning is emitted to stderr. Words missing descriptions in BOTH sources are reported on stderr at end of run so gaps stay visible. No fc.py / build-artefact changes — purely tools/gen-lib-readme.py + read-only consumption of docs/reference.html. Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/hi-claude-we-did-dreamy-pie.md. DONE 2026-05-08: tools/gen-lib-readme.py extended with scan_source_descriptions() (matches `\ name — desc` lines anywhere in a .fs file, em-dash or ASCII --, skips divider lines and stack-effect comments) and parse_reference_html() (regex over <td class="word-name"> rows, decodes HTML entities, trims long descriptions to first sentence, accepts both stack-effect and constant-value middle cells). Source comments win over reference.html with a docs-drift warning on disagreement; words with no description from either source are listed on stderr. Layout: per-file description list (with flow-control notes like has DO/LOOP appended in italic parens) followed by a narrow 5-column cost table (Word/Stack/Kind/Bytes/Cycles). Verified: cls-black sourced from reference.html; KEY-HELD? case-insensitive match works for both metrics and descriptions; snd-zap drift correctly flagged after adding a corrective source comment to lib/sound.fs; standalone fallback (no metrics JSON) still works. 16 words remain undocumented (#493). reference.html snd-zap accuracy followup at #494. Synthetic main filtering at #495.

summary: lib: add descriptions for 16 undocumented words

Either add a one-line `\ name — short description` immediately above each definition (preferred — co-located with code) or add a row to docs/reference.html. Some of these may be private-by-convention and could be renamed with a leading underscore instead. DONE 2026-05-08: added `\ name — desc` source-comment lines for all 16 previously-undocumented words across lib/dac.fs (joy-sel-rx, joy-sel-ry, joy-sel-lx, joy-sel-ly, joy-bit), lib/rg-pixel.fs (rv, rg-init-at, rg-addr), lib/rg-text.fs (rg-type), lib/fujinet.fs (FN-OP-FUJI, FN-CMD-READY, FN-CMD-TIME, fn-cmd, fn-ready/N, fn-time/N), lib/sound.fs (snd-pause). Generator now reports zero missing descriptions; all demos still build.

Also caught and fixed snd-chirp (was "Three descending tones" — but the code is three identical 60-pitch tones at the same frequency, so corrected to "Three identical high blips in quick succession — like a bird chirp."). Generator now reports zero docs-drift warnings. Added reference.html as a Makefile dep of lib/README.md so changes there trigger README regen.

summary: gen-lib-metrics.py: filter synthetic `main` from output
description: The unified-build stub at build/lib-metrics.fs ends with `: main ; main` so fc.py has something to compile. The resulting `main` word leaks into build/lib-metrics.json as a forth_word with bytes/cycles. It is harmless (no lib file defines `main`, so it never appears in lib/README.md), but it is noise: shows up in the "missing descriptions" warning, inflates the metrics word count, and is confusing if anyone inspects the JSON. Fix in tools/gen-lib-metrics.py by stripping `main` from the JSON after fc.py writes it, OR add a fc.py flag like --omit-main to skip it. A minor polish. DONE 2026-05-08: tools/gen-lib-metrics.py now post-processes the JSON to drop the synthetic main entry that the stub injects (only re-writes the file if main was actually present, so noop if fc.py changes ever stop emitting it). forth_words count went from 66 to 65; main no longer leaks into anything that consumes lib-metrics.json.

summary: kernel/boot-animation.html: support ROM-mode + all-RAM-mode toggle
description: Animation describes only the all-RAM boot dance (CLOADM staged kernel at $1000, bootstrap at $0E00, copy to $E000) — but ROM mode is now the default kernel profile (kernel ORGs at $2000, no bootstrap, no staging copy, app at $3000, ROMs stay alive at $8000-$DFFF). Update plan: add a mode toggle at the top of the page (ROM mode | All-RAM mode), maintain separate narrative step sequences and memory-map region positions for each mode, and correct the all-RAM addresses that have shifted since the animation was first written (APP_BASE, font/sin/stack offsets per current CLAUDE.md memory map). Keep the file at kernel/boot-animation.html. Cross-check addresses against kernel/README.md and CLAUDE.md memory map sections. IMPLEMENTED 2026-05-08 (awaiting browser test): added a top-of-page mode toggle (ROM mode default | All-RAM mode); body.mode-rom and body.mode-allram CSS classes drive visibility of mode-specific memory regions, narrative step boxes, address labels, and DECB record tables. ROM-mode narrative is 3 steps (Power On → LOADM → JMP START), all-RAM is 5 steps (Power On → LOADM staging → bootstrap enables all-RAM → copy $1000→$E000 → JMP START). Memory map shows kernel at $2000-$2EAF + app at $3000 + font at $5800 + sin at $7800 + stacks at $7E00/$8000 in ROM mode; staged kernel at $1000, bootstrap at $0E00, app at $2000-$8FFF, font at $9000, stacks at $DE00/$E000, kernel-final at $E000+ in all-RAM mode. JS restructured around per-mode steps/undos/stepIds objects. Mode switch rewinds the current mode to step 0 before swapping. Subtitle updated to mention both profiles. Refined per user feedback: corrected all-RAM app extent to $2000-$7FFF (was $8FFF, past LOADM ceiling); split BASIC ROM into ECB ($8000-$BFFF) and Disk-ECB/Cart ($C000-$DFFF); added explicit data-stack and return-stack zones with growth-direction labels (Data ↓ U=$7E00, Return ↓ S=$8000 in ROM mode; equivalents at $DE00/$E000 in all-RAM); added all-RAM sin-table region ($86CC); labeled font/sin blocks; expanded static-data label to call out font-data/sin-data/sprite-data/kernel-buffer contents; added heap region in ROM mode showing free space between font and sin; added $C000 address label; corrected DECB record tables (no fictional separate font/sin records — kernel bakes them in); narratives now explicitly mention LOADM ceiling at $7FFF. DONE 2026-05-08: chart flipped to standard convention (low memory at bottom, high memory at top, matching CoCo Technical Reference page 7); BASIC + DECB ownership at boot now visible with distinct regions for system DP/vars, line buffer, default text VRAM, DECB disk system, BASIC program area, and BASIC 6809 stack at $7E00-$7FFF; LOADM step hides BASIC vars/program/DECB but keeps BASIC stack alive (BASIC is the program executing the load); kernel/bootstrap takeover step retires the BASIC stack; ROM split into three correct 8K regions (ECB $8000-$9FFF, CB $A000-$BFFF, Cart $C000-$DFFF); explicit data-stack and return-stack zones with growth-direction labels in both modes; copy-arrow direction reversed to point UP from $1000 to $E000 in flipped layout.

summary: Sound: 1-bit output demo + lib primitives (snd-tone1, snd-click1, snd-noise1)
description: Build a new demo exercising the CoCo's 1-bit sound output at $FF22 bit 1 (independent of the 6-bit DAC). Add snd-tone1 ( pitch duration -- ), snd-click1 ( -- ), and snd-noise1 ( delay duration -- ) to lib/sound.fs using read-modify-write (LDB/EORB #$02/STB) to preserve VDG mode bits 7-3 and RS-232 bit 0 in $FF22. Reuse existing snd-init (sound enable is shared via $FF23 bit 3, CB2). New demo at src/sound1bit/ with key map mirroring src/sound/sound.fs (1-4 tones, 5 sweep, 6 click train, 7 noise, 8 A/B vs DAC, BREAK exit). README + auto-generated lib/README.md refresh. Crucial fix discovered during testing: PIA1 PB bit 1 needs its DDR set as output — BASIC's init leaves it as input — so snd-init now flips $FF23 bit 2 to enter DDR mode, ORs $02 into $FF22, then returns $FF23 to data mode + sound enable ($3C). Demo at src/sound1bit/ verified in XRoar: tones 1-4 clean, sweep clean, click train buzzy, noise hiss, A/B (key 8) clearly distinguishes DAC vs 1-bit timbre. Screen text intact (RMW preserves VDG mode bits). reference.html and lib/README.md updated.

summary: Add CoCo 3 intro / GIME architecture guide
description: New coco-guides/coco3-intro.md documenting the CoCo 1/2 -> 3 hardware evolution. Spine: the GIME (ACVC/TCC1014) inserts a layer of indirection + programmability under everything (color->palette, memory->MMU, timing->programmable timer) rather than replacing the 6809E/DAC/keyboard. Includes stark A->B comparison table, subsystem-by-subsystem dev-approach deltas (memory/MMU, video modes, palette RGB-vs-composite, 80-col text, keyboard, sound timing, CPU speed, RGB out), and sourced history/lore (Siegel sound-chip-vs-timer quote, Deluxe CoCo origin, Aug-1985 Microware prototype, two GIME revisions Tequila/Tortilla, myths: hidden 256-color mode, composite regression, timer +2/+1 bug, 210-line bug, Project 256 reverse-engineering, rushed Super Extended BASIC). Plus a forward-looking 'what this means for CoCo Renovation' fork section. Supporting edits: register doc in RESOURCES.md + CLAUDE.md mirrored table; new external sources appended to legends/references.md (Lomont hw ref, Sock's GIME register reference, Tandy CoCo 3 Service Manual, Siegel interview, Marentes 256 Mode, tlindner GIME timer tests).

summary: Write CoCo 3 support plan (proposals/COCO3_SUPPORT_PLAN.md)
description: Document a phased plan for explicitly supporting the CoCo 3 / GIME, grounded in a kernel+lib hardware inventory. Frames gaps in three tiers: (1) pure additions with no resisting code (GIME detection keystone, 64-color palette $FFB0-$FFBF, stable double-speed $FFD8/9, RGB-vs-composite palette tables); (2) gaps blocked by baked-in CoCo 1/2 assumptions (32-col text welded into EMIT/CR/AT/TYPE + NSCR, hardware text attributes, MMU paging $FFA0-$FFAF vs flat 64K, new graphics modes 320x192x16/640x192x2 vs RG6-only with #32 stride / #$03 color baked into rg-pset/rg-line/spr-draw/beam-trace/rg-char); (3) timing story (programmable GIME timer $FF94/95 FIRQ vs the kernel's permanent ORCC #$50 IRQ mask + all-polling design; double-speed silently breaks cycle-timed sound). Non-gaps noted: keyboard matrix + 6-bit DAC output are identical. Principle: every feature gates on runtime GIME detection so one binary still runs on a CoCo 2. References coco-guides/coco3-intro.md. Companion to ROADMAP.md (candidate future phase).

summary: Display mode reference comparing CoCo 1/2 (VDG) and CoCo 3 (GIME) text + video modes
description: New coco-guides/display-modes.md: a unified reference that puts the CoCo 1/2 VDG modes and the CoCo 3 GIME modes side by side. Text modes (VDG 32-col alphanumeric/SG vs GIME 32/40/64/80-col with attribute byte), graphics modes (VDG SG4-24/CG1-6/RG1-6 vs GIME 320/640 wide x 2/4/16 color), with resolution/colors/VRAM/bytes-per-row and the controlling registers for each (SAM $FFC0-$FFC5 + PIA1 $FF22 for VDG; GIME $FF98/$FF99 HRES/CRES + $FF9D-E offset for GIME). Cross-references vdg-modes.md (deep 1/2 detail) and coco3-intro.md (context). Needs exact GIME $FF99 bit encodings verified against Sock's GIME reference + Lomont before publishing.

summary: Implement async-sound.fs — cooperative HSYNC-polling sound library
description: General-purpose non-blocking sound library (not Space Warp specific). Cooperative cyclic-executive design: NO kernel changes — snd-poll reads HSYNC flag $FF01 bit7 and writes the 6-bit DAC $FF20 directly from a library CODE word; no FIRQ vector, no kvar-firq, no ORCC/ANDCC. Supersedes the proposal's Option C FIRQ path, which rests on a hardware error (HSYNC is on the IRQ line, not FIRQ — coco_technical_reference.txt:4775, vdg-modes.md:180-181; per-line IRQ would cost ~11,500cy/frame just in entry/exit tax). Three tiers of words: (setup) snd-async-init [CODE, reuse lib/sound.fs snd-init PIA mux->DAC]; (control, main loop) snd-note (freq amp frames--), snd-stop (--), snd-frame (--) once/VSYNC duration+envelope, snd-pitch! (freq--), snd-amp! (amp--), snd-playing? (--f); (sub-frame core) snd-poll (--) [CODE workhorse: HSYNC-edge-detect via snd-last-hs, advance snd-phase by snd-inc, lookup snd-wave[phase_hi], shift by snd-amp, STA $FF20], snd-fill (n--) [CODE tight loop emitting n HSYNC-locked samples for vsync-wait / wait-past-row spin]; (data/helpers) freq>inc (freq--inc), snd-wave 256-byte sine via DATA[PY (square special-cased). DP-located state cells in free $0050-$007F: snd-cur, snd-phase, snd-inc, snd-amp, snd-frames, snd-active, snd-last-hs. Target sample rate 16-32/frame (~480-720Hz square ceiling per space-warp #485) = fresh DAC write every ~700-1375cy, NOT every HSYNC. Open design decision to settle during impl: snd-poll call-paced vs HSYNC-edge-paced (latter chosen — stable pitch, <=84cy blocking wait). Additive: existing synchronous lib/sound.fs untouched. Refs proposals/SOUND_ENGINE_PROPOSAL.md (revision 2026-06-09) + proposals/hsync_polling_budget_chart.html.

summary: src/sound-async demo — exercise and verify async-sound.fs
description: Standalone demo that proves the async engine is non-blocking and is the test vehicle for #508 (library must be tested before #508 can resolve). Runs a moving SG4 sprite plus a continuous tone simultaneously, demonstrating that snd-poll/snd-fill placement keeps the tone going without stopping the animation. Sweeps pitch via snd-pitch! to show continuous control, and uses snd-fill inside the VSYNC-wait to show the dense-cluster venue. ROM mode, 32K. Wire into Makefile DEMOS + dsk copy (SNDASYNC.BIN). Verify in XRoar: tone is audible and steady while the sprite moves; BREAK exits to BASIC. Captures real snd-poll cycle cost measured with fc.py --cycles for feedback into the proposal/Space-Warp plan.

summary: Release Bare Naked Forth 1.2 — async sound headline feature
description: Bump the distribution version 1.1 -> 1.2 with the cooperative async sound engine (lib/async-sound.fs, #508; src/sound-async demo, #509) as the headline feature. Changes: kernel/kernel.asm KERN_VERSION EQU $0101 -> $0102 (BCD major.minor; flows to the 'BARE NAKED FORTH <version>' banner in src/hello via the fc.py-exposed KERN-VERSION constant); README.md title 'Version 1.1' -> '1.2' plus a short release note listing async sound; rebuild BOTH kernels (make + make allram) per the kernel-change rule so kernel.map carries the new symbol; rebuild hello + sound-async and re-sync demo bins. Verify hello prints 'BARE NAKED FORTH 1.2' in XRoar.

summary: Document async-sound.fs in reference.html (Library Words)
description: Add an 'Asynchronous Sound' ref-section under Library Words in reference.html, matching the existing 'Sound Effects' (sound.fs) section: INCLUDE path, prose explaining the cooperative non-blocking model (HSYNC-flag polling at $FF01, DAC at $FF20, no kernel changes, snd-fill for HSYNC-locked bursts vs snd-poll opportunistic, freq>inc assumes full snd-fill rate), and a ref-table of the public words (snd-async-init, snd-note, snd-stop, snd-frame, snd-pitch!, snd-amp!, snd-playing?, snd-poll, snd-fill, freq>inc) with stack effects, plus a short usage example. reference.html is the canonical full reference per CLAUDE.md; lib/README.md (auto-generated index) already updated under #508.

summary: async-sound: per-frame pitch slide (snd-slide!) for sweeps/zaps
description: Add a signed per-frame phase-increment delta to lib/async-sound.fs so a voice can slide pitch over its lifetime with no per-frame work by the caller. New VARIABLE snd-slide (0 = steady); snd-frame adds snd-slide to snd-inc each frame; snd-note zeroes snd-slide (fresh note steady by default); new word snd-slide! ( delta -- ). Enables one-shot sweeps (maser/zap = start high + negative slide; rise = positive slide) where the scheduler only calls snd-note + snd-slide! on the fire event and the existing per-frame snd-frame self-sweeps. Caller sizes |slide|*frames < inc0 to keep the increment positive. Convenience snd-sweep (auto-compute slide from f0/f1/frames) deferred (needs signed divide). Update reference.html + lib/README.

summary: sound-async demo: model sound-demo effects (maser/zap, beep, hit, rise)
description: Rework src/sound-async/sound-async.fs from a single steady tone into an interactive FX demo mirroring src/sound/sound.fs, but non-blocking: keypresses (Z maser/zap, B beep, H hit, R rise) start async effects via snd-note + snd-slide!; the main loop emits audio with snd-fill while a note plays (snd-playing?) and vsync-paces when idle, with a marker animating continuously to prove the game keeps running during sound. BREAK quits. Demonstrates the async equivalents of the synchronous snd-zap/snd-beep/snd-hit. Noise effects (snd-boom) need a noise wavetable, deferred.

summary: async-sound: waveform select, rest (silence), and noise
description: Engine additions to lib/async-sound.fs so the demo set can match sound.fs: (1) three more 256-byte signed wavetables snd-square/snd-saw/snd-tri alongside the existing sine (snd-wave), plus snd-waveform ( addr -- ) to point snd-wave-base at any of them (no hot-path change; snd-poll/snd-fill already read the cached base). (2) snd-rest ( frames -- ) = a silent voice for N frames (inc 0 + amp 8 -> wavetable sample collapses to DAC midpoint), the async analog of snd-pause, enabling gaps between notes. (3) noise: VARIABLE snd-seed + CODE snd-noise-fill ( n -- ) writing n HSYNC-locked pseudo-random DAC samples via a 16-bit galois LFSR (tap $B400), seeded in snd-async-init. Update reference.html + lib/README.

summary: sound-async demo: full sound.fs effect menu (waveforms, sweep, noise, chirp, dock)
description: Expand src/sound-async to mirror the src/sound menu, non-blocking: square tones (4 pitches), descending sweep, sawtooth, triangle, sine, noise burst, beep, zap/maser, boom, chirp, dock, hit. Composite effects sequenced via a small in-demo note queue (nq-reset/nq-add/nq-play over a DATA scratch buffer): each loop iteration starts the next queued note when the voice goes idle, with rests queued as silent notes (snd-rest semantics). Noise/boom driven by a chunked snd-noise-fill + a noise-frame counter so the marker keeps moving. Marker animates throughout to prove non-blocking; BREAK quits.

summary: async-sound: generate wavetables at runtime into caller-chosen RAM
description: Replace the four static DATA[PY wavetables (snd-wave/snd-square/snd-saw/snd-tri, ~1KB baked into every binary) with runtime generators that fill a 256-byte signed table at any caller-given address: gen-sine, gen-square, gen-saw, gen-tri ( addr -- ), plus /wave (256) constant. Lets 64K apps (Space Warp et al.) build tables in free hi RAM (e.g. $9200 in all-RAM mode) instead of consuming program space; apps with memory to spare can generate into a DATA buffer. snd-async-init no longer sets a default sine (no static table); caller generates a table and points snd-waveform at it. Sine uses a two-lobe parabolic approximation (cheap, no sin dependency, smooth on the 6-bit DAC). Update src/sound-async to generate its tables at startup (demo uses a fixed hi address to mirror the SW use case) and reference them via wt-* variables; update reference.html + lib/README.

summary: Oscillator/wavetable roadmap notes from Faust library review
description: proposals/OSCILLATOR_NOTES.md: forward-looking notes (post-1.2) distilled from the Faust oscillators library (faustlibraries.grame.fr/libs/oscillators), filtered for the 6809 / 6-bit DAC / ~15.7kHz constraints. Captures (1) splitting wavetable generation into its own lib/wavetable.fs separate from the async playback engine; (2) the generate-time vs play-time cost lens; (3) prioritized feature notes: variable-duty pulse/PWM, band-limited additive tables + octave mip-maps (anti-aliasing), Casio-CZ phase distortion for brightness sweeps, hard sync, resonator/coupled-form table-free sine, phasor/phase-offset exposure. Roadmap/notes only; no code.

summary: Expand OSCILLATOR_NOTES.md: 2-op/Electribe model, LFO matrix, 6809 MUL ring-mod, drum recipes
description: Major expansion of proposals/OSCILLATOR_NOTES.md driven by a 'state-of-the-art on 6809, low RAM + low CPU' design goal. Adds: design north star + RAM/CPU budget framing; a compact 2-operator+LFO voice model (one shared 256B sine table + two phase accumulators); two-operator modulation & the Korg Electribe model (ring mod, phase-mod/FM with ratio+depth, AM, sync); LFO modulation matrix (shapes incl. sample-&-hold via LFSR x targets pitch/amp/duty/PD-index/mod-depth); 6809-specific techniques (hardware MUL ring mod -- the 6502/Z80 can't; dual index regs for 2-op lookup; self-feedback FM); drum & SFX recipe cookbook (kick/tom/snare/hat/cowbell/clap/laser); wildcards (NES short-mode LFSR noise, bit/rate crush, wavefolding, wave-sequencing, Karplus-Strong, supersaw). Keeps the generate-time vs play-time lens. Roadmap/notes only.

summary: Split wavetable generators into lib/wavetable.fs
description: Move the waveform-table generators out of lib/async-sound.fs into a new dependency-free lib/wavetable.fs (the first step in OSCILLATOR_NOTES.md sequencing): /wave constant + gen-sine/gen-square/gen-saw/gen-tri. async-sound.fs keeps only the playback engine and no longer defines the generators. The two libs are independent (wavetable.fs needs only kernel primitives); the demo includes both. Update src/sound-async INCLUDEs, reference.html, and lib/README. Verify the FX demo still builds and runs identically.

summary: sound.fs: consolidate DAC/PIA init (drop per-effect re-init)
description: lib/sound.fs inlines the same PIA/DAC setup (FF01/FF03/FF21/FF23) in snd-tone, snd-tone1, snd-click1, snd-saw, snd-sin -- ~5-6 copies of ~10-15 bytes -- plus the standalone snd-init. Require callers to run snd-init once up front (matching how async-sound requires snd-async-init) and delete the inlined copies. Pure code-size win (~60-75 bytes), no CPU cost, no fc.py changes. Behavior change: effects assume snd-init has run -- document it and update the sound demos.

summary: async-sound: algorithmic waveform mode (table-free saw/square/tri)
description: gen-saw stores i-128, which is exactly the phase high byte; square is its sign; triangle is a fold of it. Only sine/custom needs a real 256-byte table. Add a per-voice waveform mode so saw/square/tri are computed inline from the phase (no table generated or stored) -- saves up to 768 bytes for apps using only simple waves. Costs one branch per emitted sample. Serves the low-RAM goal and folds into the 2-op design (sine-table operators, algorithmic carriers). Trade-off: a non-uniform emit path; weigh against keeping everything table-based.

summary: fc.py: shared-asm / macro support for CODE words
description: fc.py has no way to share assembly across CODE words: they are NEXT-terminated (not JSR-able) and there is no shared-asm prelude or macro facility. Add a mechanism -- e.g. a shared ASM[...]ASM prelude block emitted once before the CODE words where MACROs or RTS-terminated subroutines with global labels can be defined and referenced from CODE/KCODE blocks. Enabler for DRY-ing the async emit core (#523) and benefits the whole project (any repeated asm fragment). Tooling change; needs care with the local-label scoping and the combined-assembly model. [DEFERRED 2026-06-10: fc.py extracts CODE words individually, so JSR subroutines need a two-pass address-fixup; macros would DRY source but not shrink the binary. Deferred per user -- keep emit fragments inline for now; revisit if the 2-op build bloats.]

summary: async-sound: DRY the emit core (phase-advance, scale-output, hsync-wait)
description: Three fragments are copy-pasted across the emitters: phase-advance+table-lookup (snd-poll, snd-fill), amplitude-scale+recenter+mask+DAC-write (snd-poll, snd-fill, snd-noise-fill), and the HSYNC-edge wait (snd-fill, snd-noise-fill). This duplication multiplies with every future 2-op/ring-mod/phase-mod variant. Factor into shared macros or JSR-able subroutines (depends on #522). Reduces code size now and turns the 2-op build from copy-paste into compose. Keep snd-poll's hot path fast (emit only runs on a fresh HSYNC, so a JSR is amortized). [DEFERRED 2026-06-10: fc.py extracts CODE words individually, so JSR subroutines need a two-pass address-fixup; macros would DRY source but not shrink the binary. Deferred per user -- keep emit fragments inline for now; revisit if the 2-op build bloats.]

summary: async-sound: page-aligned wavetable lookup (CPU)
description: The per-sample lookup does TFR A,B / CLRA / LDY base / LDA D,Y (~20 cy) because 6809 accumulator-offset indexing is signed. If wavetables are page-aligned (table base low byte = 0, which $9200/$7000 already satisfy), the lookup becomes poke-index-into-low-byte + indirect (~12 cy) -- about 7-8 cy/sample saved, doubling in value with a second operator. Requires page-aligned tables (make it a documented constraint) and a DP pointer cell. Measure with fc.py --cycles; must stay ROM-safe (no self-modifying code).

summary: async-sound: DP-locate voice state (CPU)
description: Voice state (snd-phase/inc/amp/frames/...) lives in app RAM via FVAR_ extended addressing. Moving it into the free direct-page region ($0050-$007F) saves ~1 cy per 16-bit access in the hot emitters (~4 cy/sample, doubling under 2-op). Needs a way to place Forth VARIABLEs (or the CODE-referenced cells) at DP addresses and reference them with direct addressing. Measure the actual saving; coordinate with #524 (the lookup pointer would also be DP). [DEFERRED 2026-06-10: needs DP-variable placement -- a fc.py feature (place a VARIABLE at $0050-$007F + reference it with direct addressing in CODE) or hacky hardcoded DP addresses that break the snd-phase @ Forth API. Same tooling class as the deferred #522. CPU is already net-neutral after #521+#524; DP gets more valuable alongside the 2-op build, when it would pair with the tooling. Deferred.]

summary: wavetable: one canonical true sine from the kernel sin table
description: Two sine sources exist: gen-sine (parabolic approximation) and the kernel's real 91-byte sin-data (via lib/trig.fs init-sin, used by sound.fs snd-sin). Offer a gen-sine variant that builds the 256-byte table from the kernel sin-data by quadrant symmetry -- a truer sine, reusing bytes the kernel already ships, unifying the two sources. Keep the parabolic version as the no-dependency default; the HQ version pulls in the kernel sin table.
